evidence: `src/domains/generation/types/generationViewRow.ts` combines persisted-domain data (`PersistedGenerationRow`) with mapper-derived view fields and UI-only optimistic overlay types in one shared contract file, then `src/domains/generation/types/shotViewRow.ts` depends on that mixed type for `images`.

`src/shared/lib/errorHandling/handleError.ts` is a high-fan-in runtime facade (called out in the packet) that mixes normalization, logging, toast-notification policy, and callback dispatch in one shared entrypoint.

`src/integrations/supabase/client.ts` and `src/shared/hooks/mobile/index.ts` are global fan-in facades; they improve call-site stability but increase architecture sensitivity to changes at these single points.
No production import-time side effects were observed in the assigned runtime files (no module-scope I/O, timers, network calls, or global mutation).

Task payload builders maintain parallel schema layers: `TravelPayloadCore` and `TravelBetweenImagesOrchestratorPayload` duplicate most fields, while also embedding `task_payload_contract` (`src/shared/lib/tasks/travelBetweenImages/payloadBuilder.ts`).

`joinClips` repeats similar dual representations (`JoinClipsBaseOrchestratorDetails`/`JoinModeOrchestratorDetails` plus `JoinClipsTaskPayload`) and then wraps the same data again inside `task_payload_contract` (`src/shared/lib/tasks/joinClips.ts`).

`taskParamsUtils` compensates for schema drift with repeated fallback chains across `task_payload_contract`, `orchestrator_details`, `full_orchestrator_payload`, and top-level keys (`src/shared/lib/taskParamsUtils.ts`).

`JoinClipsPage` acts as a broad integration adapter, wiring many hook outputs and building large prop bags with per-field lambdas for `JoinClipsSettingsForm`, which creates glue-heavy handoffs (`src/tools/join-clips/pages/JoinClipsPage.tsx`).

Boundary translation is spread across UI and task layers: page-level camelCase fields are mapped manually, while payload generation applies independent mapping/default rules (`src/tools/join-clips/pages/JoinClipsPage.tsx`, `src/shared/lib/tasks/joinClips.ts`).

Video/image lightbox pipelines each perform similar state-slice assembly and panel orchestration through separate render-model paths, increasing seam surface area (`src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/ImageLightbox.tsx`, `src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/hooks/useVideoLightboxRenderModel.tsx`).

`useSegmentSlotMode` is a large mixed-responsibility hook (UI reducer, route deep-link handling, structure-video overlap mutation, pair navigation, debounced async updates) with broad dependency coupling (`src/tools/travel-between-images/components/ShotImagesEditor/hooks/useSegmentSlotMode.ts`).

Segment lightbox navigation/state logic is partially duplicated between `useSegmentSlotMode` and `useSegmentLightboxState` (`src/tools/travel-between-images/components/ShotImagesEditor/hooks/useSegmentSlotMode.ts`, `src/shared/components/ShotImageManager/ShotImageManagerContainer.tsx`).

Large coordinator files (`taskParamsUtils`, `individualTravelSegment`) rely on long fallback/normalization chains and many helper branches, making local control flow harder to reason about (`src/shared/lib/taskParamsUtils.ts`, `src/shared/lib/tasks/individualTravelSegment.ts`).

findings.summary: Generation type contracts mix persistence, derived view data, and UI-optimistic concerns, creating cross-layer coupling in domain models.
evidence: `GenerationRow` is defined as `PersistedGenerationRow & GenerationDerivedFields`, while `GenerationUiRow` adds optimistic UI state in the same file.
`ShotViewModel.images` is typed as `GenerationRow[]`, pulling mapper/view-layer fields into shot-level domain types.
findings.suggestion: Split contracts by ownership boundary: keep persistence-domain row types separate from read-model/view-model types and from UI overlay types; have `Shot` reference a persistence-oriented generation type (or explicit read model) rather than a mixed contract.

findings.summary: A single shared error facade centralizes multiple runtime policies, making many domains depend on one mixed-responsibility hub.
evidence: `reportRuntimeError` both normalizes/logs and decides toast display (`showToast ?? appError.showToast`) before invoking notification behavior.
The same function also supports callback chaining (`onError?.(appError)`), combining reporting, UX policy, and control-flow hooks in one public path.
findings.suggestion: Decompose into explicit layers: a pure normalization/logging primitive, and a separate app-level notifier/composer that decides whether to toast; keep the shared entrypoint thin and policy-free to reduce fan-in coupling.

findings.summary: Task payload contracts are represented in parallel layers, creating duplicate schema definitions and drift-prone interfaces.
evidence: `TravelBetweenImagesOrchestratorPayload` repeats nearly all `TravelPayloadCore` fields while also carrying `task_payload_contract` and family/view contracts.
`JoinClipsTaskPayload` similarly wraps an `orchestrator_details` object and contract metadata while retaining top-level compatibility fields.
`taskParamsUtils` must read from multiple locations (`task_payload_contract`, `orchestrator_details`, `full_orchestrator_payload`, top-level), indicating no single authoritative runtime interface.
findings.suggestion: Define one canonical persisted task-params schema per family (contract + authoritative details), then migrate readers/writers to that schema via explicit versioned adapters and remove fallback chains once migration completes.

findings.summary: Join Clips UI-to-task handoff is glue-heavy, with manual field adaptation spread across page orchestration and payload builder.
evidence: `JoinClipsPage` builds large `clipSettings` and `motionConfig` objects with many one-off `updateField` lambdas, tightly coupling UI wiring to settings storage keys.
`joinClips` applies separate normalization/mapping/default rules (`camelToSnakeKeys`, `mapDefinedPayloadFields`, conditional setters), so transformation logic exists on both sides of the seam.
Small utility functions in form utilities are isolated, but the broader seam still requires page-level imperative adaptation rather than one shared boundary mapper.
findings.suggestion: Introduce a dedicated JoinClips boundary adapter module that maps form state <-> task params in one place (typed DTO in/out), and have both page and task builder consume that adapter contract.

findings.summary: `useSegmentSlotMode` combines multiple unrelated concerns, producing dense control flow and fragile dependency coupling.
evidence: The hook contains reducer state management, router deep-link effects, timeline/video overlap resolution, async frame update debouncing, and view-model construction in a single file.
`useSegmentSlotModeData` depends on full `props` and `state` objects in one `useMemo`, increasing incidental recomputation and coupling.
Segment navigation responsibilities overlap with `useSegmentLightboxState` in `ShotImageManagerContainer` (next/prev, slot lookup, current media derivation), indicating duplicated low-level behavior.
findings.suggestion: Split into focused hooks (`useSegmentSlotRouting`, `useSegmentSlotNavigation`, `useSegmentStructureVideoRules`, `useSegmentSlotViewModel`) and extract shared segment-navigation helpers used by both slot-mode and shot-manager paths.

findings.summary: Image and video lightbox pipelines use parallel render-model assembly patterns that add indirection without strong reduction in complexity.
evidence: Both image and video flows build near-identical state slices (`core/media/variants/navigation`), then repackage via `useLightboxStateValue` before layout consumption.
Controls panel construction is split across separate render-model hooks with large parameter surfaces and repeated variant/task-panel wiring patterns.
`LightboxLayout` still depends on multiple context slices and many conditional branches, so additional model layers are not materially reducing UI branching complexity.
findings.suggestion: Extract a shared lightbox render-model factory for common slice assembly and action wiring, leaving only media-specific deltas as plugins; reduce duplicated slice mapping and panel wiring paths.

findings.summary: Generation→task mapping and generation transforms disagree on `tasks` payload shape
evidence: `parsePrimaryTaskId` in `generationTaskRepository` accepts only arrays of strings and marks any non-array as `invalid_tasks_shape`.
`extractTaskId` in `generationTransformers` accepts both `string` and `string[]`, so a row can be considered valid in one boundary and invalid in another.
`generationTaskCache` relies on repository status/mapping, so this mismatch can suppress task prefetch even when transformer logic could resolve a task id.
findings.suggestion: Define one shared decoder for the `generations.tasks` column (including legacy `string` and current `string[]` forms), reuse it in both repository and transformer paths, and add a focused test matrix for all accepted/rejected shapes.

findings.summary: Join-clips metadata paths expose different error and recovery contracts for duration loading
evidence: `clipInitService` uses `getVideoDurationFromUrl` and converts metadata failures to `0` duration without structured error context.
`clipManager/metadata` wraps the same concern in `OperationResult` with explicit `errorCode`, `policy`, and `recoverable` semantics.
These parallel seams make downstream orchestration behavior dependent on call path rather than a single subsystem contract.
findings.suggestion: Route all join-clips duration loading through one metadata service (preferably the `OperationResult` path), then adapt callers (including pending-clip consumption) to handle a single, explicit success/failure contract.
